content.full_text:
Susan M. Schectman
City Attorney
(415)738-7307
FAX (415) 359-6038

Office of the City Attorney
City of Pacifica
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, California 94044

BY FACSIMILE: (916) 327-2026

February 25, 1991

Jill Stecher
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice Letter - Councilmember John Schneider

Dear Ms. Stecher:

As we discussed by telephone today, I confirm the following
facts regarding this advice letter:

Councilmember Schneider owns no interest in Marc Realty
Corporation. His Loan Schedule E on his Statement of Economic
Interest is correct in that he did receive a loan of over
$10,000 in value from Marc Realty.

He does have a less than 10% ownership interest in Schneider
Four Corporation.

Lastly, the date escrow closed on the Lighthouse Hotel sale
was April 3, 1990.

I hope these facts assist you in completing the advice letter
we requested.

Very truly yours,

SUSAN M. SCHECTMAN
City Attorney

SMS:fd

cc: John Schneider

Office of the City Attorney
City of Pacifica
Susan M. Schectman
City Attorney
(415) 875-7307

170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, California 94044

April 18, 1990

Mr. John McLean
Chief of Enforcement
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Investigation of Potential Conflict of Interest
and Issuance of Advice Letter

Dear Mr. McLean:

This letter requests two actions on the part of the Fair Political
Practices Commission: (1) An investigation as to whether Pacifica
City Councilmember John Schneider possessed a conflict of interest
when he participated in the approval of a redevelopment project; and, (2) Issuance of an advice letter indicating whether
Councilmember Schneider must refrain from participating in future
actions concerning the redevelopment project in question.

Based on the limited and unverified information available to me at
the present time, the following discussion summarizes some of the
pertinent facts:

Councilmember Schneider is employed as a real estate agent for Marc
Realty Company in the City of Pacifica. In late December, 1989,
a contract of sale was entered into for the Lighthouse Hotel in
the Rockaway Beach area within the City of Pacifica. Mr. Schneider
served as the agent for the seller.

On March 12, 1990, the Pacifica Redevelopment Agency met to
consider an application for approval of a use permit, site
development permit, coastal development permit, and sign permit for
a 13,250 square foot commercial project containing fifteen (15)
retail shops. The redevelopment project in question is located
adjacent to the Lighthouse Hotel. As a City Councilmember, Mr.
Schneider also sits as a member of the governing body for the
Redevelopment Agency, and he participated in the debate and voted
to approve the requested permits, which passed on a 4-1 vote.

On March 28, 1990, an article appeared in the Pacifica Tribune
indicating that Mr. Schneider had served as a "business consultant"
with respect to the sale of the hotel. This article caused both
the public and the City Council to raise the question whether
Mr. Schneider's participation in the approval of the redevelopment
project posed a conflict of interest due to his involvement in the
sale of the Lighthouse Hotel.

John McLean
April 18, 1990
Page 2


Escrow closed on the sale in early April, and Mr. Schneider
presumably received his real estate commission at that time.

At a City Council meeting on April 9, 1990, interested citizens and
members of the City Council requested an FPPC investigation of the
question whether Mr. Schneider's past conduct posed a conflict of
interest.  In addition, it was requested that the apple be asked
to render advice concerning the issue whether Mr. Schneider is
required to abstain from participation in future decisions
concerning the redevelopment project in question. On the latter
question, please inform me as to what specific information will be
needed to enable the FPPC to issue an advice letter.

Enclosed with this letter are the following documents:

     1.  Staff report prepared for the March 12, 1990 public
         hearing before the Pacifica Redevelopment Agency.

     2.  Articles from March 28, April 4, and April 11, 1990 issues
         of the Pacifica Tribune.

     3.  March 30, 1990 Form 721 filed by Councilmember Schneider.

     4.  April 17, 1990 letter from Mr. Jeremiah F. Hallisey,
         attorney for Councilmember Schneider.

The above documents are submitted to provide background information
only; no attempt has been made to verify the accuracy of the
factual information contained therein.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter; please do not
hesitate to contact me if you require any further information.

                                         Very truly yours,


                                         [illegible]
                                         CRAIG LABADIE
                                         Acting City Attorney

Enc.

cc:  John Rothman, Legal Division
     Mayor Loeb and Councilmembers
     Dan Pincetich, City Manager

Law Offices
A Professional Corporation                    HALLISEY AND JOHNSON                    415-433-5300
                                             300 Montgomery Street
                                             San Francisco, California 94104


                                             September 7, 1990


Jill Stetcher, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

                              Re: Schneider, John

     Dear Ms. Stetcher:

          You may or may not recall that you and I talked on July
16 regarding Mr. Schneider and the request for an opinion from the
FPPC.

          After we spoke, I was of the opinion that you thought it
would be best that the opinion requested of the FPPC be delayed
until the matters being investigated by Mr. McLeare were completed.
Apparently you have told Michele Kenyon, the acting City Attorney
of Pacifica, that this is not the case.

          My notes of our July 16 conversation indicate that you
wanted a lot of information prior to the opinion letter being
issued, including such basic information as to who was requesting
the opinion, Mr. Schneider, this law firm, or the City Attorney's
office.

          As you know, Pacifica's City Attorney has been on
maternity leave. I believe that some of the problems that we have
had in communicating, (though they might all be able to be laid at
my feet), result from the fact that there have been two interim
acting City Attorneys both of whom only knew a portion of what was
going on. The City Attorney has returned from her maternity leave,
and perhaps among the three of us we can get to the bottom of this
situation.

          Is it possible that you submit to me in writing any
questions you have regarding John Schneider's affairs or
involvement with the Light House Hotel sale. That might be the
easiest way to do it. If you do not have time to submit those
questions in writing, perhaps you could call me after you have

Jill Stetcher, Esq.
September 7, 1990
Page Two


reviewed your file and you could give me over the phone a list of
questions to which I will respond in writing.

Your assistance in this matter would be greatly
appreciated.

Very truly yours,

CHARLENE HAUGHT JOHNSON

CHJ/bkm

cc: John Schneider


Ms. Stetcher
I didn't return to the office
after adjournment Fri. Sorry
children get in mail
CHT.

SENT BY:Hallisey & Johnson    ; 4-17-90 10:40AM ;        4154335300->                    ATTACHMENT 4


                                    Law Offices
A Professional Corporation        HALLISEY AND JOHNSON                    415-433-5300

                                300 Montgomery Street
                            San Francisco, California 94104


                                 April 17, 1990


Susan Schectman, Esq.
City Attorney, City of Pacifica
170 Santa Maria
Pacifica, CA  94044

Via Fax  #555-3579

Craig Labadie, Esq.
McDonough, Holland & Allen
1999 Harrison St., Ste. 1300
Oakland, CA  94612

Via Fax 839-9104

                    Re: John Schneider
                        Redevelopment Agency, City of Pacifica
                        Allegations of Conflict of Interest

Dear Counsel:

        Pursuant to your request, I have prepared a brief
statement of facts and statement of issue with regard to Mr.
Schneider's vote on the proposed development of Site "B" at the
March 12, 1990 meeting of the Redevelopment Agency.  It is my
understanding that this material will be forwarded to the FPPC for
their formal comment as to whether or not a conflict of interest
existed. Needless to say, Mr. Schneider believed that he was not
in conflict at the time he participated in the vote, and, as you
know, has always endeavored to avoid even the appearance of
conflict when involved in his public duties.

        Statement of Facts. Mr. Schneider is a member
of the five-person City Council of the City of Pacifica.
He also serves on the five-member Redevelopment Agency of
the City of Pacifica. By profession, he is a real estate
agent, and an employee of Marc Realty, City of Pacifica.

        In his capacity as an employee of Marc Realty,
Mr. Schneider participated in and received a commission
on the sale of the Lighthouse, a hotel located less than
300 feet from site "B". That commission, which was set
at the time the contract of sale was signed (on or about
December 28, 1989), was paid by the seller of the
property. Escrow closed on or about April 3, 1990. The

SENT BY:Hallisey & Johnson ; 4-17-90 10:40AM ;        4154335300-J        N * 9 INE:H 3


Susan Schectman
Craig Labadie
April 17, 1990
Page 2


     contract contained no inspection or other contingencies
whereby the buyer (or seller) could reneage on the deal or
modify its terms, including commissions.

     On March 12, 1990, the Redevelopment Agency, of
which Mr. Schneider is a member, voted on whether or not
to accept a development proposal for Redevelopment Site
"B", which is now essentially a vacant lot.  Mr.
Schneider was part of the 4-1 majority that approved the
project.  The plan called for commercial development of
the property, including several restaurants and retail
establishments.  The Lighthouse (the subject of the
above-described sale) itself also contains at least one
restaurant that is open to the public.

     Both the Lighthouse and Redevelopment Site "B"
are within the general redevelopment area which was
established several years ago.  The defining or
redefining of that general redevelopment area--its
boundaries, etc.--was not the subject of the vote on
March 12.

     Statement of Issues. 1. Was it a conflict of
interest for Mr. Schneider to have participated in the
above-described vote on March 12, 1990? 2. Should Mr.
Schneider recuse himself from all other votes concerning
the development of Site "B"?

     Please let me know if you desire additional information.

                                   Yours sincerely,


                                   [signature]
                                   JEREMIAH F. HALLISEY

JFH/sg

sections.facts:
. Mr. Schneider is a member of the five-person City Council of the City of Pacifica. He also serves on the five-member Redevelopment Agency of the City of Pacifica. By profession, he is a real estate agent, and an employee of Marc Realty, City of Pacifica.

In his capacity as an employee of Marc Realty, Mr. Schneider participated in and received a commission re the sale of the Lighthouse, a hotel located less than 300 feet from Site "B". That commission, which was set at the time the contract of sale was signed (on or about December 28, 1989), was paid by the seller of the property. Escrow closed on or about April 3, 1990. The

Susan Schectman  
Craig Labadie  
April 17, 1990

contract contained no inspection or other contingencies whereby the buyer (or seller) could renege on the deal or modify its terms, including commissions.

On March 12, 1990, the Redevelopment Agency, of which Mr. Schneider is a member, voted on whether or not to accept a development proposal for Redevelopment Site "B", which is now essentially a vacant lot. Mr. Schneider was part of the 4-1 majority that approved the project. The plan called for commercial development of the property, including several restaurants and retail establishments. The Lighthouse (the subject of the above-described sale) itself also contains at least one restaurant that is open to the public.

Both the Lighthouse and Redevelopment Site "B" are within the general redevelopment area which was established several years ago. The defining or redefining of that general redevelopment area--its boundaries, etc.--was not the subject of the vote on March 12.

Statement of Issues. 1. Was it a conflict of interest for Mr. Schneider to have participated in the above-described vote on March 12, 1990? 2. Should Mr. Schneider recuse himself from all other votes concerning the development of Site "B"?

Please let me know if you desire additional information.

Yours sincerely,

[signature]
JEREMIAH F. HALLISEY

JFH/sg
